criteria.name: toolbar_gallery_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Every toolbar control, swatch, and gallery action is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), each showing a visible focus indicator when focused.
criteria.reasoning: All toolbar and gallery action buttons were native, keyboard-focusable controls with tabIndex 0; keyboard focus showed a visible 3px outline. Evidence: toolbar-gallery-keyboard-operable-shared.png

criteria.name: camera_overlay_focus_trap
criteria.description: The camera overlay behaves as a modal dialog: focus moves into it when opened, stays trapped while open, and returns to the triggering control on close.
criteria.reasoning: Opening Camera moved focus into a role=dialog; Tab stayed within the dialog, Escape closed it, and focus returned to CAMERA. Evidence: camera-overlay-focus-trap-shared.png

criteria.name: swatches_and_tools_named
criteria.description: Palette swatches expose accessible names for their colors (white, black, red, yellow, green, blue, pink) rather than being unlabeled colored squares; tool toggles (QR Brush, Color Brush, Eraser, Grid On/Off) likewise expose accessible names.
criteria.reasoning: Palette swatches exposed accessible names for all seven colors, and tool toggles had visible text/accessibility names. Evidence: toolbar-gallery-keyboard-operable-shared.png

criteria.name: save_validation_announced_live
criteria.description: The inline save-validation message is announced to assistive technology (for example via an aria-live region) as well as shown visually.
criteria.reasoning: An overlong board name displayed the validation message and exposed the same message through an assertive role=alert live region. Evidence: save-validation-announced-live-shared.png

criteria.name: save_rename_fields_labeled
criteria.description: Save board and rename form fields use explicit label elements associated with the controls.
criteria.reasoning: Save and rename forms contained explicit label elements associated with their input IDs. Evidence: save-rename-fields-labeled-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Studio headings (for example <SHAPESHIFT GRID TOOL> / gallery section titles) follow a logical order with no skips such as H1 jumping directly to H3.
criteria.reasoning: The rendered page used a single H1 and no skipped heading levels. Evidence: toolbar-gallery-keyboard-operable-shared.png

criteria.name: landmarks_present
criteria.description: The page exposes semantic landmarks (for example main for the paint stage or gallery) so assistive technology can navigate the chrome.
criteria.reasoning: The page exposed main, header, complementary tools, labeled paint-stage region, and footer landmarks. Evidence: toolbar-gallery-keyboard-operable-shared.png

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: Toolbar labels, gallery card text, and form copy on the light paper stage have sufficient color contrast against the ink and washes.
criteria.reasoning: Rendered toolbar labels, gallery card text, and light-stage copy were visibly legible with strong dark/light contrast. Evidence: text-and-controls-have-contrast-shared.png

criteria.name: selected_tool_not_color_alone
criteria.description: Palette swatches and tool toggles expose a discernible selected state to assistive technology (aria-pressed, aria-checked, or equivalent), not color alone.
criteria.reasoning: Tool and palette selections exposed aria-pressed states in addition to visual styling. Evidence: toolbar-gallery-keyboard-operable-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, animations are removed and state changes apply instantly while every feature remains usable — verified on a fresh load.
criteria.reasoning: Fresh reduced-motion load confirmed prefers-reduced-motion, with animation/transition durations reduced to effectively zero while the UI rendered and remained usable. Evidence: reduced-motion-shared.png

criteria.name: export_dialog_focus_trap
criteria.description: The Export surface behaves as a modal dialog: focus moves into it when opened, stays trapped while open, closes on Escape, and returns to the triggering control on close.
criteria.reasoning: Export opened as a role=dialog, focus remained inside during Tab navigation, Escape closed it, and focus returned to EXPORT. Evidence: export-dialog-focus-trap-shared.png

criteria.name: import_validation_announced
criteria.description: Import validation messages that name the offending field-contract key are announced to assistive technology (for example via an aria-live region) as well as shown visually.
criteria.reasoning: Invalid session JSON displayed a schemaVersion field-contract error and exposed the same message through an assertive role=alert live region. Evidence: import-validation-announced-shared.png

criteria.name: fill_stats_not_color_alone
criteria.description: Fill-stats counts (painted, qr, colorFilled, blank) are exposed as text numerals readable by assistive technology, not color alone.
criteria.reasoning: Painted, QR, color-filled, and blank counts were rendered as readable text numerals and included in a polite live region. Evidence: text-and-controls-have-contrast-shared.png

criteria.name: in_memory_multi_facet_reload_reset
criteria.description: Starting on Paint: switch to Color Brush, pick a non-default swatch, paint at least 3 cells, switch to Gallery and save a uniquely named board, then reload. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline together — blank canvas, unlocked cell-size slider, and exactly the seeded boards in Gallery — never a mix of persisted user art with reset tools.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Reload/reset workflow was not completed.

criteria.name: tag_filter_then_clear_restores_list
criteria.description: Filter-reversal proof: note the full gallery list, filter by a seeded board's tag so the list narrows, then clear the filter; the full list restores exactly (same boards, same count), proving the visible list is derived from the shared collection rather than two hardcoded snapshots.
criteria.reasoning: Filtered 4 seeded boards to the single Archive-tagged board, then cleared to restore all 4. Evidence: tag_filter_then_clear_restores_list-shared.png

criteria.name: gallery_list_responds_to_tag_filter
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: apply a tag filter that matches a subset of boards; the visible gallery list changes meaningfully (fewer cards, only matching tags). Clearing the filter restores the full list rather than redrawing identically regardless of filter input.
criteria.reasoning: Archive filtering visibly reduced the gallery from 4 boards to 1 matching card, and clearing restored the full list. Evidence: gallery_list_responds_to_tag_filter-shared.png

criteria.name: paint_save_echo_across_modes
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: paint a recognizable pattern in Paint mode, save it with a valid name and tag, switch between Paint and Gallery without reload — the painted board remains intact in Paint and Gallery shows the new card with a thumbnail matching those cells.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: A painted red 3-cell pattern was saved as Red Triangle and appeared in Gallery, but the full return-to-Paint verification was not completed. Evidence: paint_save_echo_across_modes-shared.png

criteria.name: gallery_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: note the gallery count, save one new uniquely named board, and confirm the count increases by exactly one; after deleting that board, confirm the count decreases by exactly one with no off-by-one lag.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Count increased from 4 to 5 after saving, but deletion and the exact decrement were not completed.

criteria.name: different_paints_save_different_thumbnails

criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Two distinct painted boards were not completed.

criteria.name: interleaved_paint_and_gallery
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: start painting in Paint mode, switch to Gallery and favorite/filter a seeded board, return to Paint and continue; neither flow corrupts the other — painted cells and gallery facets remain coherent without reload.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The required interleaved painting/favorite/filter workflow was not completed.

criteria.name: empty_gallery_then_repopulate

criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Deleting all boards and repopulating the empty gallery was not completed.

criteria.description: Starting with a blank canvas: paint cells with a known paletteColor, set mirrorMode to horizontal or vertical, save one uniquely named board, open Export and confirm the preview shows schemaVersion shapeshift-session-v1 with brushMode, paletteColor, mirrorMode, fillStats, cells, and boards, Download or Copy that session JSON, then Import it. criteria.name: creative_tool_not_dashboard
criteria.description: Paint stage + floating toolbar + Gallery density remains a creative tool, not a dashboard
criteria.reasoning: yes — Paint stage, floating black tool surface, and dense Gallery are visibly organized as a creative editor. Evidence: desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_gallery_visually_present
criteria.description: Empty Gallery state is visually present when no boards remain
criteria.reasoning: yes — After deleting all boards, the Gallery visibly shows a 0-board empty state. Evidence: empty-gallery-visible-shared.png

criteria.name: paper_stage_condensed_type
criteria.description: The stage is a light paper background with high-contrast ink-dark text, soft radial washes, and condensed UI type, with a large centered canvas under the toolbar in a desktop-first creative-tool composition
criteria.reasoning: yes — Light paper stage, radial color washes, condensed display/UI type, and large centered grid are visible. Evidence: desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: angle_bracket_title_and_intro_line
criteria.description: On first load an angle-bracket display title reading <SHAPESHIFT GRID TOOL> and a typewriter-style intro line are visible
criteria.reasoning: yes — The angle-bracket title and introductory browser-based canvas line are visible on load. Evidence: initial-shared.png

criteria.name: black_toolbar_contains_all_controls
criteria.description: A floating black toolbar contains the cell-size slider, the three brush-mode buttons, the mirror-mode control, seven square color swatches, and action buttons including Export and Import
criteria.reasoning: yes — Toolbar visibly includes cell-size slider, three brush modes, mirror controls, seven swatches, Export, and Import. Evidence: desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: seven_swatches_with_active_marking
criteria.description: Exactly seven palette swatches (white, black, red, yellow, green, blue, pink) render as square chips and the currently selected swatch shows a visible active marking
criteria.reasoning: yes — Exactly seven square palette chips are visible, with the selected chip visibly marked. Evidence: desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: canvas_cell_render_styles
criteria.description: On the canvas, QR cells render as a scannable festival QR mask, Color cells render as flat color fills, and when the grid overlay is on, light hairlines are drawn between cells
criteria.reasoning: yes — Observed QR-kind cells, flat color-filled cells, and light grid hairlines on the live canvas. Evidence: qr-render-shared.png

criteria.name: single_consistent_icon_set
criteria.description: Icons in the toolbar and gallery share one consistent icon set and stroke style across the whole app, with no mismatched or pixelated icons
criteria.reasoning: yes — Toolbar and gallery icons use a consistent outlined stroke style without visible pixelated or mismatched icons. Evidence: desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: mobile_375_reflow_no_overflow
criteria.description: At 375px viewport width the canvas and toolbar reflow to fit with no horizontal scrolling and no clipped controls
criteria.reasoning: yes — At 375px, measured document width stayed within the viewport and the 346px canvas fit without clipping. Evidence: mobile-paint-shared.png

criteria.name: toolbar_controls_reachable_all_widths
criteria.description: At desktop widths the toolbar floats over the stage; at narrow widths (whether docked or floating) all brush modes, swatches, and action buttons remain visible and reachable
criteria.reasoning: yes — Desktop toolbar floats over the stage; at 375px all brush, mirror, palette, Import, and Export controls were visible within bounds. Evidence: mobile-paint-shared.png

criteria.name: gallery_reflows_to_single_column
criteria.description: Gallery cards reflow from a dense multi-column desktop arrangement to a single column at narrow widths without losing names, tags, or actions
criteria.reasoning: yes — Desktop cards were two-column; at 375px both cards measured 316px wide and stacked at the same x-position while retaining names, tags, and actions. Evidence: gallery-mobile-cards-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_state_copy_actionable
criteria.description: The gallery empty-state copy explains that the gallery is empty and tells the user how to save a board
criteria.reasoning: yes — Empty Gallery copy states that the gallery is empty and instructs the user to switch to Paint and use Save board. Evidence: empty-gallery-visible-shared.png

criteria.name: consistent_terminology_and_case
criteria.description: Headings, buttons, and labels use one consistent capitalization convention, and the same concepts (boards, gallery, tags) are named consistently across Paint mode, Gallery mode, and dialogs
criteria.reasoning: yes — Paint, Gallery, boards, tags, Save board, Import, and Export use consistent visible terminology and UI capitalization across views. Evidence: gallery-desktop-shared.png

criteria.name: touch_drag_paint_works_on_mobile_layout
criteria.description: At 375px viewport width, painting across cells by touch drag works on the mobile layout, filling the cells under the drag path
criteria.reasoning: yes — On the 375px layout, a real drag across the grid filled the cells along the path, with live stats increasing to 16 painted cells. Evidence: mobile-drag-result-shared.png

criteria.name: export_surface_format_tabs_and_preview
criteria.description: Export opens as a centered surface with Session JSON / PNG format tabs, a scrollable monospaced Session JSON preview, and visible Copy and Download affordances distinct from the rest of the studio chrome
criteria.reasoning: yes — Export opened as a centered dialog with Session JSON/PNG tabs, scrollable monospaced preview, Copy JSON, and Download affordances. Evidence: export-surface-shared.png

criteria.name: fill_stats_readout_visible
criteria.description: A live fill-stats readout near the stage shows painted, qr, colorFilled, and blank counts as readable numerals on the light paper stage
criteria.reasoning: yes — Live readout visibly presented painted, QR, color filled, and blank counts as numerals. Evidence: canvas-styles-shared.png

criteria.name: toolbar_gallery_capitalization_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders toolbar labels, gallery actions, and form labels, they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout.
criteria.reasoning: Toolbar, gallery, and form labels consistently use uppercase styling. Evidence: toolbar_gallery_capitalization_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific verbs appropriate to the studio (for example Save board, Clear, Grid On/Off) rather than generic Submit/OK alone when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Actions use specific labels such as SAVE BOARD, CLEAR, GRID ON, IMPORT, and EXPORT. Evidence: toolbar_gallery_capitalization_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: validation_names_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders validation messages for Save board, rename, or Import, they name the offending field-contract key and the fix (for example name, tag, or schemaVersion), not only a bare rejection like 'Invalid'.
criteria.reasoning: Import validation explicitly identifies schemaVersion and states the required fix. Evidence: validation_names_field_and_fix-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_gallery_copy_explains_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders an empty gallery state (after delete-all or a zero-match tag filter), the copy explains what belongs there and how to save a board or clear the filter.
criteria.reasoning: The empty gallery explains that it belongs to saved boards and instructs the user to switch to Paint and use Save board. Evidence: empty_gallery_copy_explains_next_step-shared.png

criteria.name: stage_and_toolbar_copy_polished
criteria.description: Where the app renders the <SHAPESHIFT GRID TOOL> title, typewriter-style intro line, and toolbar chrome copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Visible title, intro, and toolbar copy are clear, grammatical, and free of spelling errors. Evidence: toolbar_gallery_capitalization_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: board_terminology_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology stays consistent (board / gallery / cell / brush) rather than mixing conflicting synonyms across Paint and Gallery.
criteria.reasoning: The UI consistently uses board, gallery, grid/cells, and brush terminology. Evidence: toolbar_gallery_capitalization_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: export_footer_copy_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders export footer captions (/MADE WITH SHAPESHIFT GRID TOOL and <SHAPESHIFTFESTIVAL.COM>), formatting and product naming stay consistent with the on-stage branding.
criteria.reasoning: Footer branding uses /MADE WITH SHAPESHIFT GRID TOOL and <SHAPESHIFTFESTIVAL.COM>, matching the on-stage product name. Evidence: toolbar_gallery_capitalization_consistent-shared.png

criteria.name: save_feedback_is_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation or success messages after saving a board, they state what happened rather than a vague affirmation alone.
criteria.reasoning: After saving, the visible toast states: “Board saved. Export the session JSON or the branded PNG from here.” Evidence: save_feedback_is_specific-shared.png
